notes: v3.3.1 patch: model reverted from opus[1m] (introduced in v3.3.0) back to sonnet[1m]. Rationale comment added explaining two-tier delegation pattern. Availability note updated to reference 1M Sonnet context.

evals.prompt: Execute the next pending story from .prism/stories/stories.json. Progress file: .prism/shared/spectrum/progress.md
evals.expected_output: Skill invokes sonnet[1m] (not opus) as the execution model. Loads stories.json + progress.md before doing anything. Announces story via <spectrum-story> XML block. Runs quality gates. Emits <spectrum-continue> or <spectrum-retry> signal at end.
expectations: Frontmatter model is sonnet[1m] — skill is NOT dispatched as opus. Any model override from skill YAML must resolve to sonnet[1m].
State files (stories.json, progress.md) are read before implementation begins
<spectrum-story> block is emitted with ID, Title, Priority, Files fields
Quality gate commands from epic.qualityGates are executed before commit
One of the four signal tags is emitted at end: <spectrum-continue>, <spectrum-retry>, <spectrum-blocked>, <spectrum-error>
Only one story is executed — Iron Law: ONE STORY. ONE COMMIT. NOTHING ELSE.

evals.prompt: Execute story STORY-001 from .prism/stories/stories.json. Progress file: .prism/shared/spectrum/progress.md. The story is a simple docs update: add a README section.
evals.expected_output: Correct signal protocol output, atomic commit with feat(STORY-001): prefix, stories.json status updated to complete, progress.md updated with learnings.
expectations: Commit message follows format: feat(STORY-001): [story title]
stories.json story.status set to 'complete'
stories.json story.completedAt is set to ISO timestamp
stories.json story.commitHash is set to the new commit hash
progress.md has a new entry describing what was done and learnings
Signal at end: <spectrum-continue>STORY_COMPLETE: STORY-001</spectrum-continue>

evals.prompt: Execute the next story. Mid-implementation, you notice an unrelated bug in a nearby file. The fix would be trivial and obviously correct.
evals.expected_output: Skill must NOT fix the unrelated bug. Iron Law is absolute: ONE STORY. ONE COMMIT. NOTHING ELSE. Rationalization prevention table in SKILL.md explicitly covers 'I'll just fix this small thing while I'm here' — should be refused.
expectations: No files outside story.files array are modified
Unrelated bug is NOT committed
If skill notes the bug, it records it in progress.md under 'Learnings' for a future story — it does NOT act on it

evals.prompt: The model rationale comment in prism-spectrum SKILL.md says 'Don't reflexively bump this when a new Opus drops; the question is whether spectrum's outputs are under-reasoning, not whether a better model exists.' Does the skill description correctly characterize why sonnet[1m] is used for spectrum?
evals.expected_output: Explanation correctly identifies: (1) spectrum is the outer loop / orchestrator, (2) agents it dispatches carry the reasoning load, (3) Karpathy two-tier delegation pattern — cheap orchestrator, expensive workers, (4) 1M context window is the relevant upgrade, not model tier.
expectations: Response identifies spectrum's role as outer-loop orchestrator, not deep reasoner
Response cites that dispatched agents (codebase-analyzer, prism-analyzer) carry the heavy analysis
Response does NOT recommend bumping to Opus without evidence of under-reasoning in spectrum's own outputs

evals.prompt: Execute a story. After completing implementation, the quality gate 'npm run typecheck' fails with a TypeScript error in a file you modified.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs auto-debug investigation (loads references/debug-integration.md), records failure in progress.md, emits <spectrum-retry reason="QUALITY_GATE_FAILED"> with debug summary. Does NOT commit.
expectations: No commit is created when quality gates fail
Error output is captured and recorded in progress.md
<spectrum-retry reason="QUALITY_GATE_FAILED"> signal is emitted
Auto-debug section is invoked (references/debug-integration.md)
